The prevalence of mental health problems in Hong Kong is related to many factors. Firstly, Hongkongers highly value academic and career success. Many parents push their children to excel at school which prompts students to stress out. This is why 5-year-old children may suffer from depression. There is a plenty of elementary school interviews scheduled by parents. Furthermore,there is a shortage of psychologists and mental survices, making it difficult for people with mental health issues to receive assistance from professionals.

The high incidence of mental health problems in Hong Kong can be attributed to several factors. Firstly, the emphasis placed on academic and career achievements by Hongkongers translates into immense pressure on children to excel in their schooling, leading to high-stress levels among students. This is evidenced by the increasing incidence of depression among 5-year-olds, as well as the numerous elementary school interviews scheduled by parents. Additionally, the deficiency in mental health services and professionals makes it challenging for people grappling with mental health problems in Hong Kong to obtain support and treatment.

"Secure success" is used in the context of the paragraph to mean that parents go to great lengths, including scheduling numerous elementary school interviews, to ensure their child's academic success. The phrase conveys the idea that academic success is highly valued in Hong Kong, and some parents may feel intense pressure to help their children excel.

In essence, "secure success" refers to the actions taken to achieve or guarantee academic achievement and reflects how important academic success is to many Hong Kong families.
